skills.name: Project Directory Scaffolding
skills.description: Creates a new project directory structure with standard files (README, setup, tests). Use when starting a new project from scratch.
skills.pattern: 1. Create root dir with README.md, setup.py/pyproject.toml. 2. Create src/ with __init__.py. 3. Create tests/ with __init__.py and a sample test. 4. Add .gitignore.
skills.example_code:
import os
os.makedirs('myproject/src', exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs('myproject/tests', exist_ok=True)

skills.name: Unit Test Pattern with Setup/Teardown
skills.description: Creates unittest test classes with setUp/tearDown, temp directories, and mock objects. Use when adding tests for a new module.
skills.pattern: 1. Create test class inheriting unittest.TestCase. 2. Use setUp for temp dirs and mock objects. 3. Use tearDown for cleanup. 4. Test happy path, edge cases, and error conditions.

skills.name: Structured Error Handling with Custom Exceptions
skills.description: Defines custom exception hierarchy with context attributes. Use when building modules that need clear, actionable error reporting.
skills.pattern: 1. Define base exception for module. 2. Add subclasses for specific failure modes. 3. Include context attributes (path, operation, original error). 4. Catch at boundaries and wrap.

skills.name: CLI Argument Parsing with argparse
skills.description: Sets up command-line argument parsing with subcommands, defaults, and help text. Use when building CLI tools.
skills.pattern: 1. Create ArgumentParser with description. 2. Add subparsers for commands. 3. Add arguments with type, default, help. 4. Parse and dispatch to handler functions.
skills.example_code:
import argparse
parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='My tool')
parser.add_argument('--verbose', action='store_true')
sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest='command')
run_p = sub.add_parser('run')
run_p.add_argument('target')

skills.name: Configuration File Management
skills.description: Loads, validates, and merges configuration from JSON/env files with sensible defaults. Use when a project needs configurable behavior.
skills.pattern: 1. Define config dataclass with defaults. 2. Load from JSON file if exists. 3. Override with environment variables. 4. Validate required fields. 5. Provide from_dict/to_dict.

skills.name: Structured Logging Setup
skills.description: Configures Python logging with formatters, handlers, and level control. Use when adding observability to a project.
skills.pattern: 1. Create logger with module name. 2. Add console handler with formatter. 3. Optionally add file handler. 4. Set level from config/env. 5. Use structured format with timestamp.

skills.name: Input Validation and Sanitization
skills.description: Validates and sanitizes user input with type checking, range bounds, and safe defaults. Use at system boundaries.
skills.pattern: 1. Check type (isinstance). 2. Check bounds/length. 3. Strip/escape dangerous chars. 4. Return validated value or raise ValueError with clear message.

skills.name: HTTP Client with Error Handling
skills.description: Makes HTTP requests using urllib with timeout, error handling, and JSON parsing. Use when calling external APIs without third-party libraries.
skills.pattern: 1. Build Request with headers. 2. Use urlopen with timeout. 3. Handle HTTPError, URLError, timeout. 4. Parse JSON response. 5. Return typed result.

skills.name: REST API Endpoint Design
skills.description: Designs RESTful API endpoints with proper HTTP methods, status codes, and JSON request/response schemas. Use when building web services.
skills.pattern: 1. Map resources to URL paths. 2. Use GET/POST/PUT/DELETE correctly. 3. Return proper status codes (200, 201, 400, 404). 4. Define JSON schemas for request/response. 5. Add error response format.
skills.example_code:
# Endpoint design pattern
# GET /api/items -> 200 [{id, name}]
# POST /api/items -> 201 {id, name}
# GET /api/items/:id -> 200 {id, name} | 404
# PUT /api/items/:id -> 200 {id, name} | 404
# DELETE /api/items/:id -> 204 | 404

skills.name: HTML Form Data Handling
skills.description: Parses and validates HTML form submissions including multipart data. Use when building web forms or processing form input.
skills.pattern: 1. Parse form fields from request body. 2. Validate required fields. 3. Sanitize text inputs. 4. Handle file uploads if multipart. 5. Return structured data or validation errors.

skills.name: Web Page Scraping with HTML Parsing
skills.description: Extracts structured data from HTML pages using standard library html.parser. Use when scraping web content without external dependencies.
skills.pattern: 1. Fetch page with urllib. 2. Subclass HTMLParser. 3. Track state in handle_starttag/handle_data. 4. Collect results in a list. 5. Return structured data.

skills.name: JSON and CSV File Parsing
skills.description: Reads and writes JSON and CSV files with proper encoding, error handling, and type conversion. Use for data import/export tasks.
skills.pattern: 1. Open file with encoding='utf-8'. 2. Use json.load or csv.DictReader. 3. Validate expected fields. 4. Convert types as needed. 5. Handle missing/malformed data gracefully.

skills.name: Safe File I/O Patterns
skills.description: Reads and writes files safely with atomic writes, temp files, and proper cleanup. Use when file corruption or partial writes are a concern.
skills.pattern: 1. Write to temp file in same directory. 2. Flush and fsync. 3. Rename atomically. 4. Use context managers. 5. Handle permissions and encoding.
skills.example_code:
import os, tempfile
def atomic_write(path, content):
    dir_name = os.path.dirname(path) or '.'
    fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=dir_name)
    try:
        os.write(fd, content.encode('utf-8'))
        os.fsync(fd)
        os.close(fd)
        os.replace(tmp, path)
    except:
        os.close(fd)
        os.unlink(tmp)
        raise

skills.name: Data Schema Validation
skills.description: Validates data dictionaries against expected schemas with type checks and required fields. Use when processing external data.
skills.pattern: 1. Define schema as dict of field->type. 2. Check required fields present. 3. Check types match. 4. Collect all errors before raising. 5. Return validated data.

skills.name: Subprocess Execution with Timeout
skills.description: Runs external commands via subprocess with timeout, output capture, and error handling. Use when integrating with CLI tools.
skills.pattern: 1. Use subprocess.run with capture_output=True. 2. Set timeout. 3. Check returncode. 4. Decode stdout/stderr. 5. Raise on failure with stderr context.

skills.name: API Authentication Patterns
skills.description: Implements common API auth patterns: API keys, bearer tokens, basic auth. Use when integrating with authenticated APIs.
skills.pattern: 1. Load credentials from env/config (never hardcode). 2. Add auth header to requests. 3. Handle 401/403 responses. 4. Support token refresh if needed.
skills.example_code:
import os
from urllib.request import Request
def auth_request(url, method='GET'):
    token = os.environ.get('API_TOKEN', '')
    req = Request(url, method=method)
    req.add_header('Authorization', f'Bearer {token}')
    return req

skills.name: Webhook Receiver Pattern
skills.description: Handles incoming webhook POST requests with signature verification and payload parsing. Use when building webhook endpoints.
skills.pattern: 1. Accept POST request. 2. Verify signature/secret header. 3. Parse JSON payload. 4. Validate event type. 5. Dispatch to handler. 6. Return 200 quickly.

skills.name: Retry with Exponential Backoff
skills.description: Retries a failing operation with exponentially increasing delays. Use for transient failures in network calls or external services.
skills.pattern: 1. Loop up to max_retries. 2. Try operation. 3. On failure, sleep(base ** attempt). 4. Optionally add jitter. 5. Raise after exhaustion.

skills.name: Simple State Machine
skills.description: Implements a state machine with defined transitions and guards. Use when modeling workflows with discrete states.
skills.pattern: 1. Define states as enum/constants. 2. Define transition table {(from, event): to}. 3. Add guard conditions. 4. Implement transition method. 5. Track history.

skills.name: Pipeline Stage Orchestration
skills.description: Chains processing stages with error handling, progress tracking, and resume support. Use when building multi-step data or task pipelines.
skills.pattern: 1. Define stages as callable list. 2. Run each stage with input from previous. 3. Save state after each stage. 4. On failure, log and optionally skip/retry. 5. Support resume from last completed stage.

skills.name: Interactive User Prompting
skills.description: Prompts users for input with validation, defaults, and confirmation. Use when a workflow needs human decisions.
skills.pattern: 1. Display clear question with options. 2. Show default value. 3. Validate input format. 4. Retry on invalid input. 5. Return typed answer.

skills.name: Human Escalation Flow
skills.description: Escalates decisions to a human when automated processes cannot resolve them. Provides context, options, and handles the response. Use when automation hits an ambiguous or high-risk decision point.
skills.pattern: 1. Detect escalation trigger (confidence low, budget exceeded, auth needed). 2. Format context for human (what happened, what options exist). 3. Present choices (skip, retry, abort, custom). 4. Handle response and resume workflow.
skills.example_code:
def escalate(context, ask_fn):
    question = f'Automated process needs help:\n{context}\nWhat should we do?'
    options = ['skip', 'retry with hint', 'abort']
    decision = ask_fn(question=question, options=options)
    return decision
